title: sairocarpus multiflorus
intro: Sairocarpus multiflorus, also known as the multiflorous tooth-awl or multiflorous toothpick, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's medicinal properties also include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits such as reducing fever, and treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Sairocarpus multiflorus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to alleviate respiratory problems and promote overall well-being.
